reactions.label: value_modeled
reactions.interest: parental rights, tradition, merit and talent development
reactions.public_good_frame: a plural society needs plural schools; excellence is a public asset
reactions.argument: Reform admissions if you must, but merit must remain the basis — SES quotas replace one injustice with another.
reactions.condition_to_change: Merit-preserving instruments only (blind grading, wider access to preparation).

reactions.label: value_modeled
reactions.interest: parental rights, tradition, merit and talent development
reactions.public_good_frame: a plural society needs plural schools; excellence is a public asset
reactions.argument: Shrinking the tracks by decree punishes families for the state's failure to lift the general schools.
reactions.condition_to_change: Would reconsider if general-school quality demonstrably reached the tracks' level.

reactions.label: value_modeled
reactions.interest: parental rights, tradition, merit and talent development
reactions.public_good_frame: a plural society needs plural schools; excellence is a public asset
reactions.argument: Abolishing structured tracks sacrifices talent development to an egalitarian experiment run on other people's children.
reactions.condition_to_change: A guaranteed, well-funded talent system inside the comprehensive school could change this.

reactions.label: value_modeled
reactions.interest: parental rights, tradition, merit and talent development
reactions.public_good_frame: a plural society needs plural schools; excellence is a public asset
reactions.argument: Raise the general schools without restricting anyone's choice — improvement through addition, not prohibition.
reactions.condition_to_change: Compensation must not become a pretext for later restriction.
